name: pysimdjson
documentation:
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pysimdjson.svg?style=flat-square)
![Tests](https://github.com/TkTech/pysimdjson/workflows/Run%20tests/badge.svg)

# pysimdjson

Python bindings for the [simdjson][] project, a SIMD-accelerated JSON parser.
If SIMD instructions are unavailable a fallback parser is used, making
pysimdjson safe to use anywhere.

Bindings are currently tested on OS X, Linux, and Windows for Python version
3.9 to 3.12.

## 📝 Documentation

The latest documentation can be found at https://pysimdjson.tkte.ch.

If you've checked out the source code (for example to review a PR), you can
build the latest documentation by running `cd docs && make html`.

## 📈 Benchmarks

pysimdjson compares well against most libraries. The full benchmarks can be
found in its sister project [json_benchmark][].

[simdjson]: https://github.com/lemire/simdjson
[json_benchmark]: https://github.com/tktech/json_benchmark

modules.name: simdjson
documentation: High-level bindings for the simdjson project.

functions.name: simdjson.load
documentation:
Same as the built-in json.load(), with the following exceptions:

    - All parse_* arguments are currently ignored. simdjson does not
      currently provide hooks for these, but it is planned.
    - object_pairs_hook is ignored.
    - cls is ignored.

functions.name: simdjson.loads
documentation:
Same as the built-in json.loads(), with the following exceptions:

    - All parse_* arguments are currently ignored. simdjson does not
      currently provide hooks for these, but it is planned.
    - object_pairs_hook is ignored.
    - cls is ignored.

functions.name: tests.test_element.test_json_pointer
documentation:
Ensure JSON pointers work as expected and all possible exceptions
are converted to Python types.

functions.name: tests.test_element.test_uplift
documentation: Test uplifting of primitive types to their Python types.

functions.name: tests.test_safety.test_parser_reuse
documentation:
In the simdjson DOM model, if the parser is re-used, existing Object and
Array wrappers may point either into freed memory, random memory, or the
new document. pysimdjson should prevent this.

functions.name: tests.test_object.test_object_abc_mapping
documentation:
Ensure our Object type complies with the collections.abc.Mapping
interface.

Complying with this interface requires `__iter__`, `__len__`,
`__contains__`, `__getitem__`, `keys`, `items`, `values`, and `get`.

functions.name: tests.test_object.test_object_uplift
documentation: Ensure we can turn our Object into a python dict.

functions.name: tests.test_object.test_object_mini
documentation: Test JSON minifier.

functions.name: tests.test_object.test_object_pointer
documentation: Ensure we can access an object element by pointer.

modules.name: tests.test_array
documentation: Tests for the csimdjson.Array proxy object.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_abc_sequence
documentation:
Ensure our Array type complies with the collections.abc.Sequence
interface.

Complying with this interface requires `__iter__`, `__len__`,
`__contains__`, `__getitem__`, `__reversed__`, `index`, and `count`.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_slicing
documentation: Ensure we can slice our csimdjson.Array just like a real array.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_uplift
documentation: Ensure we can turn our Array into a python list.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_mini
documentation: Test JSON minifier.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_as_buffer
documentation: Ensure we can export homogeneous arrays as buffers.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_as_buffer_ndim
documentation:
Ensure n-dimensional arrays are flattened when converting to a
buffer.

functions.name: tests.test_array.test_array_pointer
documentation: Ensure we can access an array element by pointer.

modules.name: tests.test_minefield
documentation:
Test simdjson against the JSON minefield by Nicholas Seriot.

See https://github.com/nst/JSONTestSuite for more.

functions.name: tests.test_minefield.test_parsing
documentation: Ensure all parsing tests complete as expected.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_load_str
documentation: Ensure we can load a file from disk using a string.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_load_path
documentation: Ensure we can load a file using a Path object.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_parse_bytes
documentation: Ensure we can load from byte string fragments.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_parse_str
documentation: Ensure we can load from string fragments.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_parse_empty_buffer
documentation:
Ensure trying to parse an empty buffer returns an error consistent
with attempting to parse an empty bytestring.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_unicode_decode_error
documentation: Ensure the parser raises encoding issues.

functions.name: tests.test_parser.test_implementation
documentation: Ensure we can set, get, and list the Implementation.

functions.name: tests.test_numpy.test_array_to_numpy
documentation:
Test how quickly we can load a homogeneous array of floats into a
numpy array.

functions.name: tests.test_shim._test_load
documentation: Ensure basic usage of load is the same.

functions.name: tests.test_shim._test_loads
documentation: Ensure basic usage of loads is the same.
